definition: A violent uprising against the authority of the government or state. Section 3 of the Fourteenth Amendment disqualifies from federal office persons who have engaged in insurrection or rebellion against the United States. The precise meaning of insurrection—whether it requires violence, formal organization, or direct participation—is disputed in this case.
examples: The January 6, 2021 Capitol breach, which respondents characterize as insurrection and petitioner disputes
